drugs.details: No history of illicit drug use.

occupation.current: Housekeeping at a downtown hotel (part-time, ~25-30 hrs/wk, hourly + tips)
occupation.previous: Restaurant prep cook (2018-2020), childcare for relatives (intermittent)
occupation.status: Working — minimum-wage service job, no paid sick leave or health benefits
occupation.exposures: Repetitive hand/wrist motion, cleaning chemicals (bleach, ammonia-based products), heavy lifting (linens, mattresses). Long periods of standing.

education.highestLevel: Some high school (Guatemala). Did not complete US high school after immigrating.
education.literacyConcerns: Reads and writes Spanish at high-school level; limited English literacy.

housing.status: Stable — two-bedroom apartment in East Baltimore
housing.livingWith: Mother (Rosa, age 47, also Spanish-speaking), younger brother (age 14, US-born), and aunt
housing.notes: Patient is the primary English-speaker in the household when at appointments, though prefers Spanish for healthcare conversations. Household income relies on mother's restaurant work and patient's wages.

diet.type: Traditional Guatemalan diet — tortillas, beans, eggs, rice; limited meat; some vegetables when affordable
diet.restrictions: None medical. Reports food insecurity — has skipped meals to stretch groceries in past year.

exercise.frequency: None structured. Active at work (cleaning, lifting).

sexualHistory.partners: Prior monogamous relationship 2022-2023; no current partner

immigration.status: Notes prior immigration concerns. Patient declines to share immigration status; mother explicitly does not want this documented. Has used the same physical address since 2018.
immigration.lastReturnToCountry: 2019 — 3-week visit to grandmother. No travel since.

transportation.primary: Mostly walks or takes MTA bus; relies on mother or aunt for rides to medical visits

religion: Catholic; attends Mass with mother periodically

safety.intimatePartnerViolence: Denies — directly asked using interpreter at 2019 visit

mentalHealth.depressionScreening: PHQ-2 negative at 2019 visit; not screened since.
mentalHealth.support: Mother and aunt are primary supports; small church-based social circle

notes: Cultural context: patient and family are highly motivated to access care but have faced significant barriers — language, transportation, cost, fear related to immigration documentation, and a six-month gap during the 2020 Medicaid redetermination process they were unable to navigate. Patient has missed multiple referrals due to inability to reach specialty clinics that do not accept self-pay or do not have Spanish-language scheduling.
